channel.title: Indie Frame Notes
channel.description: Fresh takes for indie filmmakers and short-film creators.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of sound
item.description: The most common early stumbles in sound, and the cheap corrections that fix them for indie filmmakers and short-film creators.

item.title: "short film festival strategy" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about short film festival strategy through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your gear questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the gear questions indie filmmakers and short-film creators keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in distribution with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest distribution adjustments that indie filmmakers and short-film creators report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: short film festival strategy
item.description: The persistent myths around short film festival strategy, stress-tested against what indie filmmakers and short-film creators actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your production in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying production project for indie filmmakers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: The state of Production in 2026
item.description: A field report on production: where things stand, and what indie filmmakers and short-film creators should do next.

item.title: Starter guide: Sound without the overwhelm
item.description: A practical on-ramp to sound for indie filmmakers and short-film creators — one repeatable workflow, no gear debt.

item.title: "indie film lighting budget" — separating signal from noise
item.description: Thousands of people search "indie film lighting budget" every month. Most of what they find is recycled. Here is what holds up.

item.title: Why indie filmmakers are rethinking Distribution
item.description: A quiet shift is underway in how indie filmmakers and short-film creators approach distribution — less maximalism, more maintenance.

item.title: Gear on a budget: what actually matters
item.description: Where money changes outcomes in gear — and the line items that are pure vanity.

item.title: What to watch next in Sound: five quiet shifts
item.description: Ignore the headlines — these are the slower currents reshaping sound for indie filmmakers and short-film creators.
